---
title: "Population by Department"
description: "Population trends for French departments, 1968 - 2023"
format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
---

> This dashboard was generated by **OpenCode** (Pickle model). The user provided the SQL query and general guidance.

It covers **100 departments** — the 96 metropolitan departments plus the four overseas _départements d'outre-mer_ (Guadeloupe, Martinique, Guyane, La Réunion). Mayotte, Saint-Pierre-et-Miquelon, Saint-Martin, Wallis-et-Futuna, Terres australes et antarctiques françaises, and Clipperton are excluded because INSEE population data does not cover them.

```{python}
#| include: false

import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px
import plotly.io as pio
from itables import show

pio.templates.default = "plotly_white"

df = pd.read_csv("data/departments.csv")

latest = df[df["year"] == df["year"].max()]
top5_codes = latest.nlargest(5, "total_population")["department_code"].tolist()
top5 = df[df["department_code"].isin(top5_codes)]
bottom10 = latest.nsmallest(10, "total_population").sort_values("total_population")
top10 = latest.nlargest(10, "total_population").sort_values("total_population")

total_pop = int(latest["total_population"].sum())
avg_pop = int(latest["total_population"].mean())
median_pop = int(latest["total_population"].median())
most_pop = latest.loc[latest["total_population"].idxmax(), "department_name"]
most_pop_val = int(latest["total_population"].max())
least_pop = latest.loc[latest["total_population"].idxmin(), "department_name"]
least_pop_val = int(latest["total_population"].min())

df_display = df.copy()
df_display["total_population"] = df_display["total_population"].apply(lambda x: f"{x:,}")
```

## Key Metrics

```{python}
from IPython.display import display, HTML

kpi_html = f"""
<div class="kpi-row">
  <div class="kpi-card kpi-blue">
    <div class="kpi-icon">👥</div>
    <span class="kpi-value">{total_pop:,}</span>
    <span class="kpi-label">Total Population (2023)</span>
  </div>
</div>
<div class="kpi-row">
  <div class="kpi-card kpi-gold">
    <div class="kpi-icon">📊</div>
    <span class="kpi-value">{avg_pop:,}</span>
    <span class="kpi-label">Avg per Department (2023)</span>
  </div>
  <div class="kpi-card kpi-green">
    <div class="kpi-icon">📈</div>
    <span class="kpi-value">{median_pop:,}</span>
    <span class="kpi-label">Median Population (2023)</span>
  </div>
  <div class="kpi-card kpi-purple">
    <div class="kpi-icon">🏆</div>
    <span class="kpi-value">{most_pop}</span>
    <span class="kpi-label">Most Populous — {most_pop_val:,}</span>
  </div>
  <div class="kpi-card kpi-red">
    <div class="kpi-icon">📉</div>
    <span class="kpi-value">{least_pop}</span>
    <span class="kpi-label">Least Populous — {least_pop_val:,}</span>
  </div>
</div>
"""
display(HTML(kpi_html))
```

## Trends & Rankings

```{python}
fig_line = px.line(
    top5,
    x="year",
    y="total_population",
    color="department_name",
    title="Population Trends — Top 5 Departments",
    labels={"total_population": "Population", "year": "", "department_name": ""},
)
fig_line.update_layout(legend=dict(orientation="h", y=1.12))
fig_line.show()
```

```{python}
fig_bar = px.bar(
    top10,
    x="total_population",
    y="department_name",
    orientation="h",
    title="Top 10 Departments (2023)",
    labels={"total_population": "Population", "department_name": ""},
    text_auto=",.0f",
)
fig_bar.update_layout(yaxis={"categoryorder": "total ascending"})
fig_bar.show()
```

```{python}
fig_bar_bottom = px.bar(
    bottom10,
    x="total_population",
    y="department_name",
    orientation="h",
    title="Bottom 10 Departments (2023)",
    labels={"total_population": "Population", "department_name": ""},
    text_auto=",.0f",
)
fig_bar_bottom.update_layout(yaxis={"categoryorder": "total ascending"})
fig_bar_bottom.show()
```

### Yearly population by department

Search, sort, and filter all 2,300 rows of department-level population data.

```{python}
show(
    df_display,
    caption="Yearly population by department — search, sort, paginate",
    maxBytes="10MB",
    layout={"bottomEnd": "paging", "bottomStart": "info", "topEnd": "search", "topStart": "pageLength"},
)
```

<style>
.kpi-row {
  display: flex;
  gap: 1rem;
  margin: 1.5rem 0;
  flex-wrap: wrap;
}
.kpi-hero .kpi-card {
  flex: 0 0 100%;
  padding: 2.5rem;
}
.kpi-hero .kpi-value {
  font-size: 2.4rem;
}
.kpi-card {
  flex: 1;
  min-width: 180px;
  padding: 1.5rem;
  border-radius: 12px;
  text-align: center;
  color: #fff;
  transition: transform 0.2s, box-shadow 0.2s;
}
.kpi-card:hover {
  transform: translateY(-3px);
  box-shadow: 0 6px 20px rgba(0,0,0,0.15);
}
.kpi-blue   { background: linear-gradient(135deg, #4facfe, #00f2fe); }
.kpi-gold   { background: linear-gradient(135deg, #f6d365, #fda085); }
.kpi-green  { background: linear-gradient(135deg, #43e97b, #38f9d7); }
.kpi-purple { background: linear-gradient(135deg, #a18cd1, #fbc2eb); }
.kpi-red    { background: linear-gradient(135deg, #f093fb, #f5576c); }
.kpi-icon {
  font-size: 1.8rem;
  margin-bottom: 0.5rem;
}
.kpi-value {
  display: block;
  font-size: 1.6rem;
  font-weight: 700;
}
.kpi-label {
  display: block;
  font-size: 0.85rem;
  opacity: 0.9;
  margin-top: 0.25rem;
}
</style>
